category|Category:Nahaka-jinja}} [[JA:那波加神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11642755}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=October 2023}} {{神社 |名称 = Nahaka Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:那波加神社 社殿.JPG|270px]]
Main sanctuary |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907}} Noka 1-8-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802}} 7 ({{ill|lt=668|Q30116|WD=Q30116}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = Noka Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nahaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[ファイル:那波加神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] [[ファイル:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} "{{ill|lt=myōga crest|Q11617244|WD=Q11617244|lt=Daki-myōga}}"]] '''Nahaka Shrine''' (なはかじんじゃ/なばかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto}}''' '''Accompanying deity''' * {{ill|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|Q0|WD=Q0|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto}} Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3125096}}, the principal deity was said to be {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto descended here and was enshrined from ancient times. A deer appeared and carried rice seedlings to help the now-elderly deity’s farming work; this is said to be origin of shrine/place name “Noka (Nahaka/Nōka).” Nearby remain the Noka kofun cluster and Hōkō-ji kofun cluster. In {{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966}} ({{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 1), after {{ill|lt=Abo no Imao|Q11657229|WD=Q11657229|lt=Otsuki no Sukune Imao}} received Ogoto-shō, this shrine and {{ill|lt=Ogoto Shrine (Ōtsu)|Q11659231|WD=Q11659231|lt=Ogoto Shrine}} were revered as clan shrines of the {{ill|lt=Otsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133}}. Their clan temple {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Ōtsu)|Q11555028|WD=Q11555028|lt=Hōkō-ji}} became this shrine’s {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}' (Enchō 5, {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Shiga District|Q7496289|WD=Q7496289}} of {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}} as “Nahaka Shrine,” and is ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. In {{ill|lt=1571|Q6666|WD=Q6666}} ({{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064}} 2), buildings were burned in {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}’s {{ill|lt=Siege of Mount Hiei (1571)|Q10748887|WD=Q10748887}}, and rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1607|Q6756|WD=Q6756}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 12). In October {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the modern shrine rank system, promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in January {{ill|lt=1895|Q7844|WD=Q7844}}, then to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in May {{ill|lt=1902|Q2043|WD=Q2043}}. === Precincts === The main shrine is called “Lower Shrine,” versus Nahaka Aramitama Shrine called “Upper Shrine.” === Sessha and massha === === Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine) === {{神社 |名称 = Nahaka Aramitama Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:那波加荒魂神社 社殿.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = Shiga Prefecture, Ōtsu, Noka 1-9-14 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|00.47|N|135|53|18.72|E|region:JP-25|name=Nahaka Aramitama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aramitama|1=UNKNOWN}} of Nahaka Shrine |神体 = |社格 = Massha of Nahaka Shrine |創建 = {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} * Location: Noka, Ōtsu, near main shrine. * Deity: Aramitama of Nahaka Shrine. * Founded: {{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016}} (Daidō 2). === Main festivals === * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535}}) === Local information === * Nahaka Shrine (Lower Shrine): Noka 1-8-1, Ōtsu, Shiga * Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine): Noka 1-9-14, Ōtsu, Shiga ; Access * Rail: JR West {{ill|lt=Kosei Line|Q599974|WD=Q599974}}, {{ill|lt=Ogoto-Onsen Station|Q5367599|WD=Q5367599}} or {{ill|lt=Hieizan-Sakamoto Station|Q5367380|WD=Q5367380}} (about 15 min walk) * Bus: from {{ill|lt=Biwako-Hamaotsu Station|Q5644181|WD=Q5644181}} by {{ill|lt=Kojaku Kōtsū|Q11551295|WD=Q11551295}} bus (to Katata), get off at “Noka,” then 3 min walk === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), 'Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol.5 (Yamashiro/Ōmi)' (Hakusuisha, 1986) === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Sanjūbanshin|Q11354495|WD=Q11354495}} === External links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=21 Nahaka Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/037.html Nahaka Shrine | Historical Encyclopedia] - Ōtsu City Museum of History * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なはかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nahaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nahaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:小槻氏|社なはかしんしや]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

A deer appeared and carried rice seedlings to help the now-elderly deity’s farming work; this is said to be origin of shrine/place name “Noka (Nahaka/Nōka).” Nearby remain the Noka kofun cluster and Hōkō-ji kofun cluster. Their clan temple {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Ōtsu)|Q11555028|WD=Q11555028|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji Temple}} became this shrine’s {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' (Enchō 5, {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Shiga District|Q7496289|WD=Q7496289|1=Shiga District, Shiga}} of {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} as “Nahaka Shrine,” and is ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Main sanctuary |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}} Noka 1-8-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 7 ({{ill|lt=668|Q30116|WD=Q30116|1=668}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Noka Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nahaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[ファイル:那波加神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] [[ファイル:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} "{{ill|lt=myōga crest|Q11617244|WD=Q11617244|lt=Daki-myōga|1=Myōga mon}}"]] '''Nahaka Shrine''' (なはかじんじゃ/なばかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' '''Accompanying deity''' * {{ill|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|Q0|WD=Q0|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|1=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto}} Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, the principal deity was said to be {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto descended here and was enshrined from ancient times. A deer appeared and carried rice seedlings to help the now-elderly deity’s farming work; this is said to be origin of shrine/place name “Noka (Nahaka/Nōka).” Nearby remain the Noka kofun cluster and Hōkō-ji kofun cluster. In {{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}} ({{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1), after {{ill|lt=Abo no Imao|Q11657229|WD=Q11657229|lt=Otsuki no Sukune Imao|1=Abo no Imao}} received Ogoto-shō, this shrine and {{ill|lt=Ogoto Shrine (Ōtsu)|Q11659231|WD=Q11659231|lt=Ogoto Shrine|1=Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} were revered as clan shrines of the {{ill|lt=Otsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133|1=Ozuki clan}}. Their clan temple {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Ōtsu)|Q11555028|WD=Q11555028|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji Temple}} became this shrine’s {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' (Enchō 5, {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Shiga District|Q7496289|WD=Q7496289|1=Shiga District, Shiga}} of {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} as “Nahaka Shrine,” and is ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In {{ill|lt=1571|Q6666|WD=Q6666|1=1571}} ({{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} 2), buildings were burned in {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s {{ill|lt=Siege of Mount Hiei (1571)|Q10748887|WD=Q10748887|1=Siege of Mount Hiei}}, and rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1607|Q6756|WD=Q6756|1=1607}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 12). In October {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the modern shrine rank system, promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in January {{ill|lt=1895|Q7844|WD=Q7844|1=1895}}, then to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in May {{ill|lt=1902|Q2043|WD=Q2043|1=1902}}. === Precincts === The main shrine is called “Lower Shrine,” versus Nahaka Aramitama Shrine called “Upper Shrine.” === Sessha and massha === === Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine) === {{神社 |名称 = Nahaka Aramitama Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:那波加荒魂神社 社殿.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = Shiga Prefecture, Ōtsu, Noka 1-9-14 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|00.47|N|135|53|18.72|E|region:JP-25|name=Nahaka Aramitama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aramitama|1=UNKNOWN}} of Nahaka Shrine |神体 = |社格 = Massha of Nahaka Shrine |創建 = {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} * Location: Noka, Ōtsu, near main shrine. * Deity: Aramitama of Nahaka Shrine. * Founded: {{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}} (Daidō 2). === Main festivals === * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}}) === Local information === * Nahaka Shrine (Lower Shrine): Noka 1-8-1, Ōtsu, Shiga * Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine): Noka 1-9-14, Ōtsu, Shiga ; Access * Rail: JR West {{ill|lt=Kosei Line|Q599974|WD=Q599974|1=Kosei Line}}, {{ill|lt=Ogoto-Onsen Station|Q5367599|WD=Q5367599|1=Ogoto-onsen Station}} or {{ill|lt=Hieizan-Sakamoto Station|Q5367380|WD=Q5367380|1=Hieizan Sakamoto Station}} (about 15 min walk) * Bus: from {{ill|lt=Biwako-Hamaotsu Station|Q5644181|WD=Q5644181|1=Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station}} by {{ill|lt=Kojaku Kōtsū|Q11551295|WD=Q11551295|1=Kojak Bus}} bus (to Katata), get off at “Noka,” then 3 min walk === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), 'Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol.5 (Yamashiro/Ōmi)' (Hakusuisha, 1986) === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Sanjūbanshin|Q11354495|WD=Q11354495|1=Thirty Guardian Deities}} === External links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=21 Nahaka Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/037.html Nahaka Shrine | Historical Encyclopedia] - Ōtsu City Museum of History * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なはかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nahaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nahaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:小槻氏|社なはかしんしや]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 7 ({{ill|lt=668|Q30116|WD=Q30116|1=668}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Noka Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nahaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[ファイル:那波加神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] [[ファイル:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} "{{ill|lt=myōga crest|Q11617244|WD=Q11617244|lt=Daki-myōga|1=Myōga mon}}"]] '''Nahaka Shrine''' (なはかじんじゃ/なばかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' '''Accompanying deity''' * {{ill|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|Q0|WD=Q0|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|1=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto}} Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, the principal deity was said to be {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto descended here and was enshrined from ancient times. A deer appeared and carried rice seedlings to help the now-elderly deity’s farming work; this is said to be origin of shrine/place name “Noka (Nahaka/Nōka).” Nearby remain the Noka kofun cluster and Hōkō-ji kofun cluster. In {{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}} ({{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1), after {{ill|lt=Abo no Imao|Q11657229|WD=Q11657229|lt=Otsuki no Sukune Imao|1=Abo no Imao}} received Ogoto-shō, this shrine and {{ill|lt=Ogoto Shrine (Ōtsu)|Q11659231|WD=Q11659231|lt=Ogoto Shrine|1=Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} were revered as clan shrines of the {{ill|lt=Otsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133|1=Ozuki clan}}. Their clan temple {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Ōtsu)|Q11555028|WD=Q11555028|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji Temple}} became this shrine’s {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' (Enchō 5, {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Shiga District|Q7496289|WD=Q7496289|1=Shiga District, Shiga}} of {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} as “Nahaka Shrine,” and is ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In {{ill|lt=1571|Q6666|WD=Q6666|1=1571}} ({{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} 2), buildings were burned in {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s {{ill|lt=Siege of Mount Hiei (1571)|Q10748887|WD=Q10748887|1=Siege of Mount Hiei}}, and rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1607|Q6756|WD=Q6756|1=1607}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 12). In October {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the modern shrine rank system, promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in January {{ill|lt=1895|Q7844|WD=Q7844|1=1895}}, then to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in May {{ill|lt=1902|Q2043|WD=Q2043|1=1902}}. === Precincts === The main shrine is called “Lower Shrine,” versus Nahaka Aramitama Shrine called “Upper Shrine.” === Sessha and massha === === Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine) === {{神社 |名称 = Nahaka Aramitama Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:那波加荒魂神社 社殿.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = Shiga Prefecture, Ōtsu, Noka 1-9-14 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|00.47|N|135|53|18.72|E|region:JP-25|name=Nahaka Aramitama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aramitama|1=UNKNOWN}} of Nahaka Shrine |神体 = |社格 = Massha of Nahaka Shrine |創建 = {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} * Location: Noka, Ōtsu, near main shrine. * Deity: Aramitama of Nahaka Shrine. * Founded: {{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}} (Daidō 2). === Main festivals === * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}}) === Local information === * Nahaka Shrine (Lower Shrine): Noka 1-8-1, Ōtsu, Shiga * Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine): Noka 1-9-14, Ōtsu, Shiga ; Access * Rail: JR West {{ill|lt=Kosei Line|Q599974|WD=Q599974|1=Kosei Line}}, {{ill|lt=Ogoto-Onsen Station|Q5367599|WD=Q5367599|1=Ogoto-onsen Station}} or {{ill|lt=Hieizan-Sakamoto Station|Q5367380|WD=Q5367380|1=Hieizan Sakamoto Station}} (about 15 min walk) * Bus: from {{ill|lt=Biwako-Hamaotsu Station|Q5644181|WD=Q5644181|1=Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station}} by {{ill|lt=Kojaku Kōtsū|Q11551295|WD=Q11551295|1=Kojak Bus}} bus (to Katata), get off at “Noka,” then 3 min walk === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), 'Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol.5 (Yamashiro/Ōmi)' (Hakusuisha, 1986) === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Sanjūbanshin|Q11354495|WD=Q11354495|1=Thirty Guardian Deities}} === External links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=21 Nahaka Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/037.html Nahaka Shrine | Historical Encyclopedia] - Ōtsu City Museum of History * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なはかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nahaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nahaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:小槻氏|社なはかしんしや]][[Category:qq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' '''Accompanying deity''' * {{ill|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|Q0|WD=Q0|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|1=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto}} Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, the principal deity was said to be {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto descended here and was enshrined from ancient times. A deer appeared and carried rice seedlings to help the now-elderly deity’s farming work; this is said to be origin of shrine/place name “Noka (Nahaka/Nōka).” Nearby remain the Noka kofun cluster and Hōkō-ji kofun cluster. In {{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}} ({{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1), after {{ill|lt=Abo no Imao|Q11657229|WD=Q11657229|lt=Otsuki no Sukune Imao|1=Abo no Imao}} received Ogoto-shō, this shrine and {{ill|lt=Ogoto Shrine (Ōtsu)|Q11659231|WD=Q11659231|lt=Ogoto Shrine|1=Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} were revered as clan shrines of the {{ill|lt=Otsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133|1=Ozuki clan}}. Their clan temple {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Ōtsu)|Q11555028|WD=Q11555028|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji Temple}} became this shrine’s {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' (Enchō 5, {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Shiga District|Q7496289|WD=Q7496289|1=Shiga District, Shiga}} of {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} as “Nahaka Shrine,” and is ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In {{ill|lt=1571|Q6666|WD=Q6666|1=1571}} ({{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} 2), buildings were burned in {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s {{ill|lt=Siege of Mount Hiei (1571)|Q10748887|WD=Q10748887|1=Siege of Mount Hiei}}, and rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1607|Q6756|WD=Q6756|1=1607}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 12). In October {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the modern shrine rank system, promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in January {{ill|lt=1895|Q7844|WD=Q7844|1=1895}}, then to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in May {{ill|lt=1902|Q2043|WD=Q2043|1=1902}}. === Precincts === The main shrine is called “Lower Shrine,” versus Nahaka Aramitama Shrine called “Upper Shrine.” === Sessha and massha === === Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine) === {{神社 |名称 = Nahaka Aramitama Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:那波加荒魂神社 社殿.JPG|250px]]

Access * Rail: JR West {{ill|lt=Kosei Line|Q599974|WD=Q599974|1=Kosei Line}}, {{ill|lt=Ogoto-Onsen Station|Q5367599|WD=Q5367599|1=Ogoto-onsen Station}} or {{ill|lt=Hieizan-Sakamoto Station|Q5367380|WD=Q5367380|1=Hieizan Sakamoto Station}} (about 15 min walk) * Bus: from {{ill|lt=Biwako-Hamaotsu Station|Q5644181|WD=Q5644181|1=Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station}} by {{ill|lt=Kojaku Kōtsū|Q11551295|WD=Q11551295|1=Kojak Bus}} bus (to Katata), get off at “Noka,” then 3 min walk === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), 'Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol.5 (Yamashiro/Ōmi)' (Hakusuisha, 1986) === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Sanjūbanshin|Q11354495|WD=Q11354495|1=Thirty Guardian Deities}} === External links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=21 Nahaka Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/037.html Nahaka Shrine | Historical Encyclopedia] - Ōtsu City Museum of History * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nahaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nahaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:大津市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:小槻氏|社なはかしんしや]][[Category:qq]]

link|Q11642755}} == Japanese content == {{脚注の不足|date=October 2023}} {{神社 |名称 = Nahaka Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:那波加神社 社殿.JPG|270px]]
Main sanctuary |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}} Noka 1-8-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 7 ({{ill|lt=668|Q30116|WD=Q30116|1=668}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Noka Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nahaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[ファイル:那波加神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] [[ファイル:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} "{{ill|lt=myōga crest|Q11617244|WD=Q11617244|lt=Daki-myōga|1=Myōga mon}}"]] '''Nahaka Shrine''' (なはかじんじゃ/なばかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' '''Accompanying deity''' * {{ill|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|Q0|WD=Q0|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|1=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto}} Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, the principal deity was said to be {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto descended here and was enshrined from ancient times. A deer appeared and carried rice seedlings to help the now-elderly deity’s farming work; this is said to be origin of shrine/place name “Noka (Nahaka/Nōka).” Nearby remain the Noka kofun cluster and Hōkō-ji kofun cluster. In {{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}} ({{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1), after {{ill|lt=Abo no Imao|Q11657229|WD=Q11657229|lt=Otsuki no Sukune Imao|1=Abo no Imao}} received Ogoto-shō, this shrine and {{ill|lt=Ogoto Shrine (Ōtsu)|Q11659231|WD=Q11659231|lt=Ogoto Shrine|1=Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} were revered as clan shrines of the {{ill|lt=Otsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133|1=Ozuki clan}}. Their clan temple {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Ōtsu)|Q11555028|WD=Q11555028|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji Temple}} became this shrine’s {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' (Enchō 5, {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Shiga District|Q7496289|WD=Q7496289|1=Shiga District, Shiga}} of {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} as “Nahaka Shrine,” and is ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In {{ill|lt=1571|Q6666|WD=Q6666|1=1571}} ({{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} 2), buildings were burned in {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s {{ill|lt=Siege of Mount Hiei (1571)|Q10748887|WD=Q10748887|1=Siege of Mount Hiei}}, and rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1607|Q6756|WD=Q6756|1=1607}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 12). In October {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the modern shrine rank system, promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in January {{ill|lt=1895|Q7844|WD=Q7844|1=1895}}, then to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in May {{ill|lt=1902|Q2043|WD=Q2043|1=1902}}. === Precincts === The main shrine is called “Lower Shrine,” versus Nahaka Aramitama Shrine called “Upper Shrine.” === Sessha and massha === === Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine) === {{神社 |名称 = Nahaka Aramitama Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:那波加荒魂神社 社殿.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = Shiga Prefecture, Ōtsu, Noka 1-9-14 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|00.47|N|135|53|18.72|E|region:JP-25|name=Nahaka Aramitama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aramitama|1=UNKNOWN}} of Nahaka Shrine |神体 = |社格 = Massha of Nahaka Shrine |創建 = {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} * Location: Noka, Ōtsu, near main shrine. * Deity: Aramitama of Nahaka Shrine. * Founded: {{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}} (Daidō 2). === Main festivals === * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}}) === Local information === * Nahaka Shrine (Lower Shrine): Noka 1-8-1, Ōtsu, Shiga * Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine): Noka 1-9-14, Ōtsu, Shiga ; Access * Rail: JR West {{ill|lt=Kosei Line|Q599974|WD=Q599974|1=Kosei Line}}, {{ill|lt=Ogoto-Onsen Station|Q5367599|WD=Q5367599|1=Ogoto-onsen Station}} or {{ill|lt=Hieizan-Sakamoto Station|Q5367380|WD=Q5367380|1=Hieizan Sakamoto Station}} (about 15 min walk) * Bus: from {{ill|lt=Biwako-Hamaotsu Station|Q5644181|WD=Q5644181|1=Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station}} by {{ill|lt=Kojaku Kōtsū|Q11551295|WD=Q11551295|1=Kojak Bus}} bus (to Katata), get off at “Noka,” then 3 min walk === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), 'Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol.5 (Yamashiro/Ōmi)' (Hakusuisha, 1986) === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Sanjūbanshin|Q11354495|WD=Q11354495|1=Thirty Guardian Deities}} === External links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=21 Nahaka Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/037.html Nahaka Shrine | Historical Encyclopedia] - Ōtsu City Museum of History * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nahaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nahaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:小槻氏|社なはかしんしや]][[Category:qq]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 7 ({{ill|lt=668|Q30116|WD=Q30116|1=668}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Noka Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nahaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[ファイル:那波加神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] [[ファイル:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} "{{ill|lt=myōga crest|Q11617244|WD=Q11617244|lt=Daki-myōga|1=Myōga mon}}"]] '''Nahaka Shrine''' (なはかじんじゃ/なばかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' '''Accompanying deity''' * {{ill|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|Q0|WD=Q0|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|1=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto}} Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, the principal deity was said to be {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto descended here and was enshrined from ancient times. A deer appeared and carried rice seedlings to help the now-elderly deity’s farming work; this is said to be origin of shrine/place name “Noka (Nahaka/Nōka).” Nearby remain the Noka kofun cluster and Hōkō-ji kofun cluster. In {{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}} ({{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1), after {{ill|lt=Abo no Imao|Q11657229|WD=Q11657229|lt=Otsuki no Sukune Imao|1=Abo no Imao}} received Ogoto-shō, this shrine and {{ill|lt=Ogoto Shrine (Ōtsu)|Q11659231|WD=Q11659231|lt=Ogoto Shrine|1=Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} were revered as clan shrines of the {{ill|lt=Otsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133|1=Ozuki clan}}. Their clan temple {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Ōtsu)|Q11555028|WD=Q11555028|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji Temple}} became this shrine’s {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' (Enchō 5, {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Shiga District|Q7496289|WD=Q7496289|1=Shiga District, Shiga}} of {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} as “Nahaka Shrine,” and is ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In {{ill|lt=1571|Q6666|WD=Q6666|1=1571}} ({{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} 2), buildings were burned in {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s {{ill|lt=Siege of Mount Hiei (1571)|Q10748887|WD=Q10748887|1=Siege of Mount Hiei}}, and rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1607|Q6756|WD=Q6756|1=1607}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 12). In October {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the modern shrine rank system, promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in January {{ill|lt=1895|Q7844|WD=Q7844|1=1895}}, then to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in May {{ill|lt=1902|Q2043|WD=Q2043|1=1902}}. === Precincts === The main shrine is called “Lower Shrine,” versus Nahaka Aramitama Shrine called “Upper Shrine.” === Sessha and massha === === Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine) === {{神社 |名称 = Nahaka Aramitama Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:那波加荒魂神社 社殿.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = Shiga Prefecture, Ōtsu, Noka 1-9-14 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|00.47|N|135|53|18.72|E|region:JP-25|name=Nahaka Aramitama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aramitama|1=UNKNOWN}} of Nahaka Shrine |神体 = |社格 = Massha of Nahaka Shrine |創建 = {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} * Location: Noka, Ōtsu, near main shrine. * Deity: Aramitama of Nahaka Shrine. * Founded: {{ill|lt=807|Q30016|WD=Q30016|1=807}} (Daidō 2). === Main festivals === * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}}) === Local information === * Nahaka Shrine (Lower Shrine): Noka 1-8-1, Ōtsu, Shiga * Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine): Noka 1-9-14, Ōtsu, Shiga ; Access * Rail: JR West {{ill|lt=Kosei Line|Q599974|WD=Q599974|1=Kosei Line}}, {{ill|lt=Ogoto-Onsen Station|Q5367599|WD=Q5367599|1=Ogoto-onsen Station}} or {{ill|lt=Hieizan-Sakamoto Station|Q5367380|WD=Q5367380|1=Hieizan Sakamoto Station}} (about 15 min walk) * Bus: from {{ill|lt=Biwako-Hamaotsu Station|Q5644181|WD=Q5644181|1=Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station}} by {{ill|lt=Kojaku Kōtsū|Q11551295|WD=Q11551295|1=Kojak Bus}} bus (to Katata), get off at “Noka,” then 3 min walk === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), 'Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol.5 (Yamashiro/Ōmi)' (Hakusuisha, 1986) === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Sanjūbanshin|Q11354495|WD=Q11354495|1=Thirty Guardian Deities}} === External links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=21 Nahaka Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/037.html Nahaka Shrine | Historical Encyclopedia] - Ōtsu City Museum of History * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nahaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nahaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:Otsuki Clan]][[Category:qq]]

Main sanctuary |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}} Noka 1-8-1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|WD=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 7 ({{ill|lt=668|Q30116|WD=Q30116|1=668}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Noka Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nahaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[ファイル:那波加神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] [[ファイル:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} "{{ill|lt=myōga crest|Q11617244|WD=Q11617244|lt=Daki-myōga|1=Myōga mon}}"]] '''Nahaka Shrine''' (なはかじんじゃ/なばかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' '''Accompanying deity''' * {{ill|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|Q0|WD=Q0|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|1=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto}} Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, the principal deity was said to be {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto descended here and was enshrined from ancient times. A deer appeared and carried rice seedlings to help the now-elderly deity’s farming work; this is said to be origin of shrine/place name “Noka (Nahaka/Nōka).” Nearby remain the Noka kofun cluster and Hōkō-ji kofun cluster. In {{ill|lt=851|Q29966|WD=Q29966|1=851}} ({{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1), after {{ill|lt=Abo no Imao|Q11657229|WD=Q11657229|lt=Otsuki no Sukune Imao|1=Abo no Imao}} received Ogoto-shō, this shrine and {{ill|lt=Ogoto Shrine (Ōtsu)|Q11659231|WD=Q11659231|lt=Ogoto Shrine|1=Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} were revered as clan shrines of the {{ill|lt=Otsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133|1=Ozuki clan}}. Their clan temple {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Ōtsu)|Q11555028|WD=Q11555028|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji Temple}} became this shrine’s {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' (Enchō 5, {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Shiga District|Q7496289|WD=Q7496289|1=Shiga District, Shiga}} of {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} as “Nahaka Shrine,” and is ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. In {{ill|lt=1571|Q6666|WD=Q6666|1=1571}} ({{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} 2), buildings were burned in {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s {{ill|lt=Siege of Mount Hiei (1571)|Q10748887|WD=Q10748887|1=Siege of Mount Hiei}}, and rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1607|Q6756|WD=Q6756|1=1607}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 12). In October {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the modern shrine rank system, promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in January {{ill|lt=1895|Q7844|WD=Q7844|1=1895}}, then to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in May {{ill|lt=1902|Q2043|WD=Q2043|1=1902}}. === Precincts === The main shrine is called “Lower Shrine,” versus Nahaka Aramitama Shrine called “Upper Shrine.” === Sessha and massha === === Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine) === {{神社 |名称 = Nahaka Aramitama Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:那波加荒魂神社 社殿.JPG|250px]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|qid=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 7 ({{ill|lt=668|Q30116|qid=Q30116|1=668}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Noka Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|qid=Q2535|1=April 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nahaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[ファイル:那波加神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] [[ファイル:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} "{{ill|lt=myōga crest|Q11617244|qid=Q11617244|lt=Daki-myōga|1=Myōga mon}}"]] '''Nahaka Shrine''' (なはかじんじゃ/なばかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|qid=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' '''Accompanying deity''' * {{ill|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|Q0|qid=Q0|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|1=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto}} Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|qid=Q3125096}}, the principal deity was said to be {{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto descended here and was enshrined from ancient times. A deer appeared and carried rice seedlings to help the now-elderly deity’s farming work; this is said to be origin of shrine/place name “Noka (Nahaka/Nōka).” Nearby remain the Noka kofun cluster and Hōkō-ji kofun cluster. In {{ill|lt=851|Q29966|qid=Q29966|1=851}} ({{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|qid=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1), after {{ill|lt=Abo no Imao|Q11657229|qid=Q11657229|lt=Otsuki no Sukune Imao|1=Abo no Imao}} received Ogoto-shō, this shrine and {{ill|lt=Ogoto Shrine (Ōtsu)|Q11659231|qid=Q11659231|lt=Ogoto Shrine|1=Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} were revered as clan shrines of the {{ill|lt=Otsuki clan|Q11462133|qid=Q11462133|1=Ozuki clan}}. Their clan temple {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Ōtsu)|Q11555028|qid=Q11555028|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji Temple}} became this shrine’s {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' (Enchō 5, {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Shiga District|Q7496289|qid=Q7496289|1=Shiga District, Shiga}} of {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|qid=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} as “Nahaka Shrine,” and is ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. In {{ill|lt=1571|Q6666|qid=Q6666|1=1571}} ({{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|qid=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} 2), buildings were burned in {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s {{ill|lt=Siege of Mount Hiei (1571)|Q10748887|qid=Q10748887|1=Siege of Mount Hiei}}, and rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1607|Q6756|qid=Q6756|1=1607}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 12). In October {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|qid=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the modern shrine rank system, promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in January {{ill|lt=1895|Q7844|qid=Q7844|1=1895}}, then to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in May {{ill|lt=1902|Q2043|qid=Q2043|1=1902}}. === Precincts === The main shrine is called “Lower Shrine,” versus Nahaka Aramitama Shrine called “Upper Shrine.” === Sessha and massha === === Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine) === {{神社 |名称 = Nahaka Aramitama Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:那波加荒魂神社 社殿.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = Shiga Prefecture, Ōtsu, Noka 1-9-14 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|05|00.47|N|135|53|18.72|E|region:JP-25|name=Nahaka Aramitama Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aramitama|1=UNKNOWN}} of Nahaka Shrine |神体 = |社格 = Massha of Nahaka Shrine |創建 = {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|qid=Q30016|1=807}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} * Location: Noka, Ōtsu, near main shrine. * Deity: Aramitama of Nahaka Shrine. * Founded: {{ill|lt=807|Q30016|qid=Q30016|1=807}} (Daidō 2). === Main festivals === * Annual festival ({{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|qid=Q2535|1=April 29}}) === Local information === * Nahaka Shrine (Lower Shrine): Noka 1-8-1, Ōtsu, Shiga * Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine): Noka 1-9-14, Ōtsu, Shiga ; Access * Rail: JR West {{ill|lt=Kosei Line|Q599974|qid=Q599974|1=Kosei Line}}, {{ill|lt=Ogoto-Onsen Station|Q5367599|qid=Q5367599|1=Ogoto-onsen Station}} or {{ill|lt=Hieizan-Sakamoto Station|Q5367380|qid=Q5367380|1=Hieizan Sakamoto Station}} (about 15 min walk) * Bus: from {{ill|lt=Biwako-Hamaotsu Station|Q5644181|qid=Q5644181|1=Biwako-Hamaōtsu Station}} by {{ill|lt=Kojaku Kōtsū|Q11551295|qid=Q11551295|1=Kojak Bus}} bus (to Katata), get off at “Noka,” then 3 min walk === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), 'Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol.5 (Yamashiro/Ōmi)' (Hakusuisha, 1986) === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Sanjūbanshin|Q11354495|qid=Q11354495|1=Thirty Guardian Deities}} === External links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=21 Nahaka Shrine] - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office * [https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/037.html Nahaka Shrine | Historical Encyclopedia] - Ōtsu City Museum of History * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nahaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nahaka Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:Otsuki Clan]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|qid=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 7 ({{ill|lt=668|Q30116|qid=Q30116|1=668}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Noka Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|qid=Q2535|1=April 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nahaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[ファイル:那波加神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] [[ファイル:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} "{{ill|lt=myōga crest|Q11617244|qid=Q11617244|lt=Daki-myōga|1=Myōga mon}}"]] '''Nahaka Shrine''' (なはかじんじゃ/なばかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|qid=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' '''Accompanying deity''' * {{ill|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|Q0|qid=Q0|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|1=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto}} Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|qid=Q3125096}}, the principal deity was said to be {{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto descended here and was enshrined from ancient times. A deer appeared and carried rice seedlings to help the now-elderly deity’s farming work; this is said to be origin of shrine/place name “Noka (Nahaka/Nōka).” Nearby remain the Noka kofun cluster and Hōkō-ji kofun cluster. In {{ill|lt=851|Q29966|qid=Q29966|1=851}} ({{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|qid=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1), after {{ill|lt=Abo no Imao|Q11657229|qid=Q11657229|lt=Otsuki no Sukune Imao|1=Abo no Imao}} received Ogoto-shō, this shrine and {{ill|lt=Ogoto Shrine (Ōtsu)|Q11659231|qid=Q11659231|lt=Ogoto Shrine|1=Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} were revered as clan shrines of the {{ill|lt=Otsuki clan|Q11462133|qid=Q11462133|1=Ozuki clan}}. Their clan temple {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Ōtsu)|Q11555028|qid=Q11555028|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji Temple}} became this shrine’s {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' (Enchō 5, {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Shiga District|Q7496289|qid=Q7496289|1=Shiga District, Shiga}} of {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|qid=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} as “Nahaka Shrine,” and is ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. In {{ill|lt=1571|Q6666|qid=Q6666|1=1571}} ({{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|qid=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} 2), buildings were burned in {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s {{ill|lt=Siege of Mount Hiei (1571)|Q10748887|qid=Q10748887|1=Siege of Mount Hiei}}, and rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1607|Q6756|qid=Q6756|1=1607}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 12). In October {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|qid=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the modern shrine rank system, promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in January {{ill|lt=1895|Q7844|qid=Q7844|1=1895}}, then to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in May {{ill|lt=1902|Q2043|qid=Q2043|1=1902}}. === Precincts === The main shrine is called “Lower Shrine,” versus Nahaka Aramitama Shrine called “Upper Shrine.” === Sessha and massha === === Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine) === {{神社 |名称 = Nahaka Aramitama Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:那波加荒魂神社 社殿.JPG|250px]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|qid=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} 7 ({{ill|lt=668|Q30116|qid=Q30116|1=668}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Noka Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 29|Q2535|qid=Q2535|1=April 29}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nahaka Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[ファイル:那波加神社 鳥居.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] [[ファイル:Japanese crest daki Myouga.svg|thumb|160px|right|{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shrine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} "{{ill|lt=myōga crest|Q11617244|qid=Q11617244|lt=Daki-myōga|1=Myōga mon}}"]] '''Nahaka Shrine''' (なはかじんじゃ/なばかじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|qid=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === '''Principal deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}''' '''Accompanying deity''' * {{ill|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|Q0|qid=Q0|lt=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto|1=Ochiwake-no-Mikoto}} Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|qid=Q3125096}}, the principal deity was said to be {{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}. === History === According to shrine tradition, Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto descended here and was enshrined from ancient times. A deer appeared and carried rice seedlings to help the now-elderly deity’s farming work; this is said to be origin of shrine/place name “Noka (Nahaka/Nōka).” Nearby remain the Noka kofun cluster and Hōkō-ji kofun cluster. In {{ill|lt=851|Q29966|qid=Q29966|1=851}} ({{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|qid=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 1), after {{ill|lt=Abo no Imao|Q11657229|qid=Q11657229|lt=Otsuki no Sukune Imao|1=Abo no Imao}} received Ogoto-shō, this shrine and {{ill|lt=Ogoto Shrine (Ōtsu)|Q11659231|qid=Q11659231|lt=Ogoto Shrine|1=Ogoto Shrine (Otsu)}} were revered as clan shrines of the {{ill|lt=Otsuki clan|Q11462133|qid=Q11462133|1=Ozuki clan}}. Their clan temple {{ill|lt=Hōkō-ji (Ōtsu)|Q11555028|qid=Q11555028|lt=Hōkō-ji|1=Hōkō-ji Temple}} became this shrine’s {{ill|lt=bettō temple|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' (Enchō 5, {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), it appears in {{ill|lt=Shiga District|Q7496289|qid=Q7496289|1=Shiga District, Shiga}} of {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|qid=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} as “Nahaka Shrine,” and is ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. In {{ill|lt=1571|Q6666|qid=Q6666|1=1571}} ({{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|qid=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} 2), buildings were burned in {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}’s {{ill|lt=Siege of Mount Hiei (1571)|Q10748887|qid=Q10748887|1=Siege of Mount Hiei}}, and rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1607|Q6756|qid=Q6756|1=1607}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 12). In October {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|qid=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the modern shrine rank system, promoted to {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in January {{ill|lt=1895|Q7844|qid=Q7844|1=1895}}, then to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in May {{ill|lt=1902|Q2043|qid=Q2043|1=1902}}. === Precincts === The main shrine is called “Lower Shrine,” versus Nahaka Aramitama Shrine called “Upper Shrine.” === Sessha and massha === === Nahaka Aramitama Shrine (Upper Shrine) === {{神社 |名称 = Nahaka Aramitama Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:那波加荒魂神社 社殿.JPG|250px]]
